findings.title: North Star Metric: Search Relevance Score
findings.description: The North Star metric for SearchBoost is the search relevance score, which measures the effectiveness of search results in meeting user intent. This metric is critical as it directly impacts user satisfaction and retention.
assumptions: Search relevance is a primary driver of user engagement and satisfaction.
recommendations: Monitor search relevance scores closely and investigate factors contributing to the drop.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Search Latency (P95)
findings.description: The P95 search latency metric measures the time taken for 95% of search queries to return results. This is crucial for user experience, especially on mobile devices.
assumptions: Lower latency leads to higher user satisfaction and engagement.

evidence.excerpt: P95 search latency on mobile web has climbed to 890ms, up from a baseline of ~420ms pre-deploy.

recommendations: Optimize the ranking model or implement mobile-specific caching to reduce latency.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Zero Result Rate
findings.description: The zero result rate measures the percentage of search queries that return no results. This metric must not degrade as it indicates potential issues in search effectiveness.
assumptions: A high zero result rate negatively impacts user experience.
recommendations: Investigate the causes of increased zero result rates and implement improvements.

metadata.measurement_method: percentage of queries returning no results

findings.title: Instrumentation Gap for New Query Types
findings.description: There is a significant gap in data collection for new query types such as image search and voice search, as no analytics events are instrumented for these types. This limits our ability to measure their performance and impact.
assumptions: Instrumentation is necessary for measuring the success of new features.

evidence.excerpt: No analytics events were instrumented for these new query types.

recommendations: Implement baseline instrumentation for image search and voice search query types.

findings.title: Establish Baseline Metrics for Image Search
findings.description: With the launch of image search, it is critical to establish baseline metrics such as query volume, zero-result rate, and click-through rate to measure its success and inform future improvements.
assumptions: Baseline metrics are essential for evaluating new features.

evidence.excerpt: We need to establish: query volume, zero-result rate, click-through rate on image results.

recommendations: Conduct an audit to establish baseline metrics for image search.

summary: The analysis reveals a critical drop in search relevance scores and increased search latency, necessitating immediate attention. Instrumentation gaps for new query types and the need for baseline metrics for image search highlight areas for improvement to ensure effective measurement and decision-making.
